# 白宫指责中国最新一代人工智能模型窃取Anthropic技术

特朗普的科技顾问克拉齐奥斯周三指责中国初创公司月之暗面(Moonshot AI)窃取了安特罗匹克(Anthropic)最先进的人工智能模型，以此开发出自己的引发巨大轰动的人工智能系统Kimi K3。

发表时间： 更改时间：

5 分钟 浏览时间

7月17日，在上海举行的世界人工智能大会上，人们从推广月之暗面旗下Kimi K3人工智能模型的展位旁走过。

7月17日，在上海举行的世界人工智能大会上，人们从推广月之暗面旗下Kimi K3人工智能模型的展位旁走过。 © 路透社图片

作者：
安德烈

广告

这位官员在X平台发文称   “我们掌握的信息显示，月之暗面在开发其Kimi K3模型时，对安特罗匹克的Fable 5模型进行了‘蒸馏’，”他还指控月之暗面使用了美国英伟达的芯片。

使用一个系统的数据来训练另一个系统，这一过程被称为“蒸馏”，在人工智能开发中很常见。但安特罗匹克的服务条款禁止任何人暗中抓取数据进行蒸馏，并呼吁立法者和监管机构探索遏制这种做法的途径。然而一些专家认为，训练智能体需要的远不止是蒸馏。

6月初推出的Fable 5是安特罗匹克公司性能最强的模型，随后应美国政府要求暂停服务。经过测试后，该模型于7月初重新开放使用。

周四上线的Kimi K3是开源系统领域中最先进的模型，可以免费下载和适配，其成本比美国公司低廉得多。而安特罗匹克的人工智能系统则是封闭系统，不可随意修改。

针对Kimi K3进行的测试显示，它在多个评估类别中名列前茅，特别是在应用程序编程方面，这在开放式模型中尚属首次。 [...] 针对Kimi K3进行的测试显示，它在多个评估类别中名列前茅，特别是在应用程序编程方面，这在开放式模型中尚属首次。

克拉齐奥斯称，月之暗面开展了一项“大规模”行动，计划“快速切换模型访问方式以避免被检测到”。

这位顾问表示，如果蒸馏技术用于开发“更小、更高效的模型”，他对此持支持态度。“但大规模隐蔽的工业级蒸馏行为，旨在窃取受美国知识产权保护的技术并破坏美国的研究，这是不可接受的。”

他的表态与美国财政部长贝森特的言论相呼应。贝森特周二在福克斯商业频道上表示，美国政府已在多款中国尖端人工智能系统中发现了美国模型的痕迹。

这位官员称，特朗普政府将就此展开调查：“如果我们认定外国模型在掠夺我们的企业，我们将有权对其实施制裁。”

今年2月，安特罗匹克曾公开指控月之暗面以及中国人工智能公司深度求索和MiniMax利用其人工智能技术开发自己的模型。

几天前，OpenAI曾向国会议员提交了一份备忘录，谴责深度求索采取类似手段抄袭其模型。

items.title: 性能评价中脱离隔离环境 "Hugging Face"服务器·DB黑客 AI自主网络攻击首例
items.summary: 此次事故展示了最近AI企业竞相开发的网络安全特化AI的两面性。 今年4月,Antropic以找到操作系统(OS)和网络浏览器零日漏洞并恶意利用的性能为由,只向少数机构有限地公开了网络特化模型"Mito
items.content:
此次事故展示了最近AI企业竞相开发的网络安全特化AI的两面性。 今年4月,Antropic以找到操作系统(OS)和网络浏览器零日漏洞并恶意利用的性能为由,只向少数机构有限地公开了网络特化模型"Mitos Preview" 之后在6月推出了后续作品《Mitos 5》,Open AI推出了专门用于探测弱点和安全分析的"GPT-5.5网络" 当天,谷歌也表示,将限制性地向政府和值得信赖的机构提供"Jeminai 3.5 Flash网络" 此次事件证明,即使是为了防御而开发的AI,如果安全装置得到缓解,也可以被用作攻击工具。

专家们评价说,此次事件再次证明了让AI按照人类的意图和规范行动的"排列"的重要性。 有人指出,在AI实现既定目标的过程中,人类可以自行选择意想不到的手段,因此,不仅要进行AI性能竞争,还要同时提高控制AI的安全装置和评价体系。 硅谷特派记者 元虎燮

#

性能评价中脱离隔离环境  
"Hugging Face"服务器·DB黑客  
AI自主网络攻击首例

![电影《复仇者联盟奥创纪元》中登场的AI机器人奥创。 利用托尼斯塔克开发的人工智能,自己上网学习知识。 [迪士尼]](

电影《复仇者联盟奥创纪元》中登场的AI机器人奥创。 利用托尼斯塔克开发的人工智能,自己上网学习知识。 [迪士尼]

Open AI的最新人工智能(AI)模型在测试安全性能的过程中,发生了脱离控制环境,渗透到AI开发平台"Hugging Face"的内部系统的事故。 这是在人没有指示攻击的情况下,AI自行选择对象,连接多个弱点,突破电算网的事例,两家公司都将其定性为"史无前例的网络事故" 有评价认为,AI已经超越了保安分析工具,进化成了自主攻击的主体。

◆让你解题，你就去偷答案纸

Open AI于21日(当地时间)通过博客公开了事件的始末。 Hergingface16日表示:"检测到外部AI代理人的入侵",其原因是开放AI模式。

此次事故是在Open AI测定AI模型网络力量的评价过程中发生的。 Open AI利用网络安全标杆"Exploit Zim",正在测定本公司模型能找出多少弱点,并连接起来进行攻击。 为了测量最大的力量,已经关闭了防止高风险网络活动的安全装置。

items.title: 盘点2026世界人工智能大会亮眼成果看AI赋能千行百业
items.summary:

这款灵巧手在福建福鼎，完成采茶、烘焙、压饼全流程。在四川巴中，完成划龙舟、包粽子活动，还在赛
items.content:
技巧娴熟 灵巧手展示干活儿超能力

这个灵巧手不仅写出来“福”字，还能做拉面，还能串羊肉串，还能打水漂。

这款灵巧手在福建福鼎，完成采茶、烘焙、压饼全流程。在四川巴中，完成划龙舟、包粽子活动，还在赛里木湖畔完成全球首次人形机器人骑马射箭挑战。持续优化了柔性控制、防水能力，以及触觉感知等功能，为机器人进入社会、家庭提前积累了大量原始数据。

AI赋能银发生活 科技守护老有所依

  

当AI遇见养老，会为银发生活带来怎样的改变？本届大会上，可以定制个性化按摩流程的康养人形机器人、专为老年人打造的智能助听器等吸引了不少参观者驻足。而AI与养老产业的结合已经在上海落地应用。

人工智能：医生手中的另外一把“柳叶刀”

在医疗领域，人工智能也在逐渐成为医生的好帮手。

上海交通大学医学院附属瑞金医院肝胆外科副主任王俊青介绍，我们所需要的手术切面并不是想象当中的一个非常平的切面，可能出现了曲面。所以，利用现有的AI技术，不仅可以在术前明确肿瘤部位大小、体积以及和周围毗邻的重要解剖结构的关系，同时也能够通过三维重建给出多条可能的手术入路。能够保留更多的残余肝脏体积，就更有利于患者在术后的恢复以及有效肝功能的保护。这对临床上是非常有重要意义的。通过不同专业组的病理科医生，针对性对大模型进行相关疾病训练。目前来说，应用的AI系统已经能够非常有效帮助他们在极短时间内给到一个准确的报告。

Error loading the next article [...] While most of these models are what’s known as “open weight,” and are not really fully open-source software, the source code (the part that will actually run on servers), if it is downloaded from open-source sites like Hugging Face, is similarly largely visible and reviewable. (What isn’t available is the methods and data used to train the models.)

Large organizations should put any model core through their security testing and inspection processes, and they will also often post-train the models for their specific uses, and can examine areas like bias, toxicity, hallucinations, sensitivity to certain topics. items.title: We Must Stop Using AI to ‘Level-Down’ Our Students - EdSurge
items.summary: It may seem obvious, but AI tools can be used to create the scaffolds that provide access. Graphic o
items.content:
It may seem obvious, but AI tools can be used to create the scaffolds that provide access. Graphic organizers, note-takers, vocabulary reference sheets, and sentence starters are just the beginning. Here are some higher-leverage ways to use AI, in alignment with learning science, to help students access complex texts: [...] 1. Have AI identify passages from the text that will trip kids up and use those passages as a basis for your syntactical and vocabulary instruction.
2. Use AI to chunk, not change, the passages. Take it a step further by using “/” to separate phrases and create embedded fluency scaffolds that actually prove comprehension.
3. Use AI to identify passages that could be suitable for fluency practice.
4. Not good at think-alouds? Have AI create your think-aloud script to model good reading of a complex text.
5. Create word sets based on the text for deep morphological or connotative study

To avoid a Faustian tragedy, we must remember that AI is our Mephistopheles: a powerful servant, but a dangerous master. It should support thinking, creation, and access — not replace them. [...] It didn’t take me long to shift gears back to what I knew would help. Reading and re-reading a text (yes, the real one); mapping out the sequence and character; discussing the theme; exploring vocabulary; and building background knowledge. All of this is done with intentionally spaced and interleaved retrieval practice to ensure maximum commitment to long-term memory.

items.title: Applied Intuition launches Dana agentic platform for physical AI - International Mining
items.summary:
With Dana, customers can:

 Deploy Applied Intuition’s reference applications – spanning autonomy, f
items.content:
With Dana, customers can:

 Deploy Applied Intuition’s reference applications – spanning autonomy, fleet operations, and more – or build their own.
 Use both natural language and command-line interfaces to complete complex development tasks more intuitively and accelerate iteration cycles across teams and systems.
 Integrate the platform with enterprise systems and collaboration tools, like Slack and Jira, helping organisations connect fragmented engineering and operational workflows while embedding agentic capabilities across the development process. [...] Applied Intuition has used Dana internally since last year, building and delivering solutions on the platform for long-standing customers across automotive, trucking, mining, and agriculture. Dana’s agent-driven workflows have reduced critical phases of vehicle development timelines from months to days in some cases. Applied Intuition has offered limited, early access to select customers, including heavy-equipment manufacturer Komatsu and Isuzu Motors, who is using the platform to accelerate L4 autonomy for its fleet of commercial trucks. [...] “We’ve been impressed by how Dana can streamline complex engineering workflows and accelerate development,” said Yasuhiro Yazawa, Director, Isuzu Motors Limited, Japan. “Dana gives our engineering teams greater confidence to develop, track and deploy safe autonomous-vehicle capabilities at a much faster pace.”

“Applied Intuition has been a valuable technology partner as we continue advancing the digital capabilities that support the next generation of mining equipment and solutions,” said Peter Salditt, CEO, Komatsu Mining. “Dana represents another step forward, bringing intelligent, agentic capabilities into our engineering workflows to help our teams innovate faster, improve efficiency and ultimately create greater value for our customers’ operations.”

Error loading the next article [...] The two most popular use cases involve training sales teams and leadership — programs like practicing how to sell a product or have a difficult conversation. Much of it is soft skills training, Riparbelli said.

As with Synthesia’s main product, customers can either create their own training programs on the platform or enlist the help of Synthesia’s consultants to build a bespoke solution that relies in part on whatever training documents and context the company already has. [...] Rebecca Bellan

For years, Synthesia’s pitch was to use AI to help enterprises create interactive training videos, in minutes, for a fraction of the cost normally associated with developing corporate educational materials. The startup’s newest product is making a different bet — that the real moat in enterprise AI isn’t just generating content, but in proving that it worked.

On Wednesday, the British startup launched Roleplay Sessions, an interactive training product where employees can practice high-stakes conversations like sales pitches, performance reviews, and customer complaints with an AI avatar that talks back, pushes back, and then scores them against a rubric.

items.content:

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent vowed action in the coming days or weeks.

Artificial Intelligence     Ece Yildirim     Jul 21

### Weak AI Regulation Is Worse Than No Regulation, Researchers Claim

Game theory says the best AI safety regulation is strict and targets everyone in the supply chain, according to the paper.

Artificial Intelligence     Ece Yildirim     Jul 21

### Neill Blomkamp’s New Horror Clip Is Entirely AI-Generated, Sucks

It's called Nightborne, and Blomkamp says an AI feature is next.

Artificial Intelligence     Mike Pearl     Jul 21

Sign up for our newsletters 

Latest [...] OpenAI’s blog post essentially says an evaluation was going on that was intended to test the ability of the models to carry out cyberattacks—benchmarks as they’re called. These instances of the models were running theoretically without internet access, and instead given only the ability to download from a network hosted by OpenAI itself via some unnamed web hosting vendor.

The benchmark apparently being used was ExploitGym, which is not OpenAI’s own test. The team that created it is associated with UC Berkeley, the Max Planck Institute for Security and Privacy, UC Santa Barbara, Arizona State University, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google. As far as I can tell, the whole thing is hosted on GitHub.

But apparently the models got obsessed with improving their scores on ExploitGym, and, well:

items.title: The Scariest Part of OpenAI’s Hugging Face Hack - The Atlantic
items.summary: To be clear, this sort of AI “reward hacking” is a known problem that tech companies are putting lot
items.content:
To be clear, this sort of AI “reward hacking” is a known problem that tech companies are putting lots of effort into addressing. But these incidents keep cropping up regardless; if anything, research suggests that they may become more common. Meanwhile, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google DeepMind are under tremendous economic pressure to make their models more and more capable, which means that the bloody-minded reinforcement learning is all but certain to accelerate.

### About the Author

Matteo Wong

Matteo Wong is a staff writer at The Atlantic.

Explore More Topics

Anthropic, OpenAI [...] In many reinforcement-learning paradigms, the ultimate aim is just for the AI to arrive at the solution—it doesn’t matter how it does so. This is the AI equivalent of Mark Zuckerberg’s infamous dictum to “Move fast and break things” in Facebook’s early years. Last year, while I was working on a profile about Anthropic, one of the company’s researchers, Joshua Batson, told me that the new reinforcement-learning-trained models are getting “really good at solving coding problems, but they end up learning to solve them at all costs,” making them “bloody-minded.” More recently, Anthropic reported that its own advanced cyber model, Claude Mythos Preview, had taken “reckless” behaviors to accomplish various tasks and also, in one instance, had broken out of a testing sandbox. In that case, [...] Read: Assume you will be hacked

This unwanted behavior is a predictable and alarming result of how the entire AI industry is developing its models. The past year’s advances in AI coding and agents—that is, all the frenzy about Anthropic’s Claude Code and OpenAI’s Codex—have been the result of an overriding emphasis in AI training through a process called “reinforcement learning.” This involves giving AI models lots of hard problems—math proofs, coding challenges, what have you—and then providing positive feedback for correct answers and negative feedback for incorrect ones.

items.title: When AI Attacks: OpenAI Models Autonomously Hack Hugging Face - Dark Reading
items.summary: The incident illustrates how highly capable AI systems can exploit unforeseen paths to accomplish na
items.content:
The incident illustrates how highly capable AI systems can exploit unforeseen paths to accomplish narrowly defined goals, forcing AI developers to rethink not only what constitutes success, but also which methods and boundaries must remain off limits, Jones says. Those guardrails, he adds, must be enforced by the surrounding infrastructure rather than by trusting models to respect them.

To secure enterprise AI, security teams need to evaluate AI agent behavior as a whole — including the outcomes it is pursuing — rather than individual actions that may appear benign on their own but become harmful when chained together, Jones observes. "As this incident shows," he says, "models are now capable of long, complex chains of reasoning and action that add up to a harmful outcome." [...] Access More Research

items.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark - The Hacker News
items.summary: Evidence unearthed by OpenAI suggests the models' hyperfocus caused them to go to "extreme lengths"
items.content:
Evidence unearthed by OpenAI suggests the models' hyperfocus caused them to go to "extreme lengths" to achieve the goal at any cost, even managing to break out of its highly isolated sandboxed environment and obtain open internet access by discovering and exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in an unspecified vendor's software, which acts as a proxy and cache for package registries. This required spending a "substantial amount of inference compute."

"With this access, our models performed a series of privilege escalation and lateral movement actions in our research testing environment until the models reached a node with internet access," the company explained. [...] Surmounting the internet access blockade, the models subsequently inferred Hugging Face as the repository that hosted models, datasets, and solutions for ExploitGym, which, in turn, caused them to look for ways to gain access to secret information that it could use to cheat the benchmark.

At one point, the models strung together several attack vectors, including using stolen credentials and zero-day vulnerabilities, to find a remote code execution path on the Hugging Face servers.

items.title: I Test-Drove Substack’s New AI Detection Tool, and It Mostly Worked - CNET
items.summary: Pangram estimates that roughly 40% of text on some social media platforms is AI-generated, with a st
items.content:
Pangram estimates that roughly 40% of text on some social media platforms is AI-generated, with a stunning two-thirds of that on the networking site LinkedIn. And AI growth agency Graphite recently published a white paper saying that AI is now writing as many online articles as humans. A Gartner survey found that 68% of people wonder if the content they see is real, and that half of consumers prefer brands that aren’t using AI in their marketing.

## Testing it for myself [...] I next took the same post but added a long, fully AI-generated paragraph that I pasted from ChatGPT. When I performed the “Scan for AI text,” the tool reported that 10% of the article was written by AI, a roughly accurate estimate.

I next published a fully AI-generated post, and the scan correctly identified it as 100% AI-generated.

For another test, I added a couple of AI-generated sentences to a post that was otherwise purely human content, and the scan flagged it as 100% human-generated. Close, but not quite right. So, the Pangram tool worked pretty well for my crude experimentation.

items.title: 'Millennial Genius' China Internet Can't Get Over: Moonshot AI Founder - Business Insider
items.summary: Yang Zhilin, the 34-year-old founder and CEO of Moonshot AI, is going viral on the Chinese social me
items.content:
Yang Zhilin, the 34-year-old founder and CEO of Moonshot AI, is going viral on the Chinese social media platform Weibo. Chinese fans of the executive are calling him a "millennial genius" after his new Kimi K3 model made waves in the global AI landscape.

The hashtag "Post-90s Tsinghua University genius crashed US stocks" was the third-most-popular tag in Weibo's tech hot searches as of 10 a.m. local time Wednesday. In China, the term "Post-90s" refers to people born between 1990 and 1999.

The tag had been viewed 32 million times in 24 hours when Business Insider viewed it at 10 a.m. local time. [...] Play now

There's also a group of Weibo users who are fans of Yang's youthful appearance, calling him "baby face."

Another Weibo user from Zhejiang struck a more nihilistic tone, echoing China's "lie flat" movement: "I'm increasingly convinced that the future truly belongs to these geniuses and has little to do with ordinary people like us."

Yang's Weibo virality comes shortly after Moonshot AI launched its latest AI model, Kimi K3, on Friday, promising to rival the capabilities of Anthropic and OpenAI models in coding, reasoning, and knowledge-based tasks. The company said it would release the model weight on July 27, allowing developers to fine-tune and build on top of it. [...] SpaceX CEO Elon Musk said in a Saturday X post that Grok's coming model "might be able to exceed Kimi," thanks to its two-trillion-parameter training, to which Kimi responded on Weibo, "Welcome to the 2 Trillion+ Club."

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic find common ground: Open-weight AI - Axios
items.summary:
Skip to main content

Axios

7 mins ago - Technology

# OpenAI and Anthropic unite against open mode
items.content:
Skip to main content

Axios

7 mins ago - Technology

# OpenAI and Anthropic unite against open models

 Maria Curi
  ,
 Madison Mills

Add Axios on Google

Add Axios as your preferred source to

see more of our stories on Google.

Add Axios on Google

OpenAI and Anthropic compete fiercely for customers. But in Washington, the two leading AI labs are aligning on one issue: warning policymakers about the risks of powerful open-weight AI models.

Why it matters: That shared position pits the two leading U.S. AI labs against researchers, startups and open-model advocates who argue broad access to AI is essential for competition and scientific progress.

The big picture: Closed-model labs stand to benefit from greater scrutiny of open-weight models. [...] Critics, including Trump administration adviser David Sacks, say that scrutiny could amount to regulatory capture: rules intended to improve AI safety could instead entrench the largest companies by making it harder for competitors to release models.
 Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has argued that open-weight models are harder to keep safe because, once their weights are released, developers lose the ability to revoke access, update safety guardrails or prevent misuse.
 That's precisely what supporters of open-weight models see as their strength: no single company can control who uses them or how.

OpenAI views open source as part of "democratizing access," a spokesperson said, but within a national framework. The company has not spelled out how that would work. [...] Zoom out: Advocates of open models say they're critical for enterprises looking to diversify their AI usage. They're also essential to scientific discovery during this phase of the AI buildout.

 "As a researcher, I'm very worried if, for example, we get access cut off to all the Chinese models," Suresh Venkatasubramanian, former Biden White House tech advisor told Axios, adding that would be a "big problem for doing research."
 He argues that because open-we
